content:
“By joining the WTO, China is not simply agreeing to import more of our products,” Clinton told the audience.
“It is agreeing to import one of democracy’s most cherished values: economic freedom.”
Open the markets. Let the money flow. And eventually, inevitably, one-party rule in Beijing would dissolve like sugar in hot water. China would grow rich and then grow free.
Now, twenty-six years later, China is the world’s second-largest economy at $19.4 trillion. It hasn’t become remotely democratic. If anything, under Xi Jinping, the Chinese Communist Party’s grip on power is tighter than it was when Clinton made that speech.
And now China isn’t just refusing to play by American rules. It’s building an entirely different game.
This is the trauma that defines American foreign policy in 2026. Not just that, China rose.
